174	// isTLSTerminateListener returns true if the listener is a TLS listener in Terminate mode.
175	func isTLSTerminateListener(l *graph.Listener) bool {
176		return l.Source.TLS != nil && (l.Source.TLS.Mode == nil || *l.Source.TLS.Mode == v1.TLSModeTerminate)
177	}
178	
179	// buildTLSServers builds TLSServers from TLSRoutes attached to TLS listeners.
180	// Both Passthrough and Terminate mode listeners are processed. Terminate mode servers
181	// include SSL configuration for TLS termination in the stream block.
182	func buildTLSServers(gateway *graph.Gateway) []Layer4VirtualServer {
183		var gatewayNsName types.NamespacedName
184		if gateway.Source != nil {
185			gatewayNsName = types.NamespacedName{Namespace: gateway.Source.Namespace, Name: gateway.Source.Name}
186		}
187		tlsServersMap := make(map[graph.L4RouteKey][]Layer4VirtualServer)
188		listenerDefaultServers := make([]Layer4VirtualServer, 0)
189	
190		tlsServerCount := 0
191	
192		for _, l := range gateway.Listeners {
193			if !l.Valid || l.Source.Protocol != v1.TLSProtocolType {
194				continue
195			}
196	
197			var ssl *SSL
198			if isTLSTerminateListener(l) {
199				ssl = buildSSL(l)
200			}
201	
202			count, matched := buildTLSServersForListener(l, ssl, gatewayNsName, tlsServersMap)
203			tlsServerCount += count
204	
205			if !matched {
206				if ds := buildTLSDefaultServer(l, ssl); ds != nil {
207					listenerDefaultServers = append(listenerDefaultServers, *ds)
208				}
209			}
210		}
211		tlsServers := make([]Layer4VirtualServer, 0, tlsServerCount+len(listenerDefaultServers))
212	
213		// Collect route keys in sorted order for deterministic output.
214		routeKeys := make([]graph.L4RouteKey, 0, len(tlsServersMap))
215		for key := range tlsServersMap {
216			routeKeys = append(routeKeys, key)
217		}
218		sort.Slice(routeKeys, func(i, j int) bool {
219			if routeKeys[i].NamespacedName.Namespace != routeKeys[j].NamespacedName.Namespace {
220				return routeKeys[i].NamespacedName.Namespace < routeKeys[j].NamespacedName.Namespace
221			}
222			return routeKeys[i].NamespacedName.Name < routeKeys[j].NamespacedName.Name
223		})
224	
225		for _, key := range routeKeys {
226			tlsServers = append(tlsServers, tlsServersMap[key]...)
227		}
228	
229		tlsServers = append(tlsServers, listenerDefaultServers...)
230	
231		// Sort for deterministic output: by port, then hostname, then defaults last.
232		sort.Slice(tlsServers, func(i, j int) bool {
233			if tlsServers[i].Port != tlsServers[j].Port {
234				return tlsServers[i].Port < tlsServers[j].Port
235			}
236			if tlsServers[i].IsDefault != tlsServers[j].IsDefault {
237				return !tlsServers[i].IsDefault // non-defaults first
238			}
239			return tlsServers[i].Hostname < tlsServers[j].Hostname
240		})
241	
242		return tlsServers
243	}
244	
245	// buildTLSServersForListener processes routes on a TLS listener, adding servers to tlsServersMap.
246	// Returns the number of servers added and whether any route hostname matched the listener hostname.
247	func buildTLSServersForListener(
248		l *graph.Listener,
249		ssl *SSL,
250		gatewayNsName types.NamespacedName,
251		tlsServersMap map[graph.L4RouteKey][]Layer4VirtualServer,
252	) (int, bool) {
253		count := 0
254		foundRouteMatchingListenerHostname := false
255	
256		for key, r := range l.L4Routes {
257			if !r.Valid {
258				continue
259			}
260	
261			var hostnames []string
262	
263			for _, p := range r.ParentRefs {
264				if val, exist := p.Attachment.AcceptedHostnames[graph.CreateParentRefListenerKeyFromListener(l)]; exist {
265					hostnames = val
266					break
267				}
268			}
269	
270			if _, ok := tlsServersMap[key]; !ok {
271				tlsServersMap[key] = make([]Layer4VirtualServer, 0)
272			}
273	
274			count += len(hostnames)
275	
276			for _, h := range hostnames {
277				if l.Source.Hostname != nil && h == string(*l.Source.Hostname) {
278					foundRouteMatchingListenerHostname = true
279				}
280				tlsServersMap[key] = append(tlsServersMap[key], Layer4VirtualServer{
281					Hostname: h,
282					Upstreams: []Layer4Upstream{
283						{
284							Name:   r.Spec.BackendRef.ServicePortReference(),
285							Weight: 0, // TLSRoute doesn't support weights
286						},
287					},
288					Port:      l.Source.Port,
289					SSL:       ssl,
290					VerifyTLS: convertBackendTLS(r.Spec.BackendRef.BackendTLSPolicy, gatewayNsName),
291				})
292			}
293		}
294	
295		return count, foundRouteMatchingListenerHostname
296	}
297	
298	// buildTLSDefaultServer creates a default server for a TLS listener if needed.
299	func buildTLSDefaultServer(l *graph.Listener, ssl *SSL) *Layer4VirtualServer {
300		hostname := ""
301		if l.Source.Hostname != nil {
302			hostname = string(*l.Source.Hostname)
303		}
304	
305		return &Layer4VirtualServer{
306			Hostname:  hostname,
307			IsDefault: true,
308			Port:      l.Source.Port,
309			Upstreams: []Layer4Upstream{},
310			SSL:       ssl,
311		}
312	}
313	
314	// buildL4Servers builds Layer4 servers (TCP or UDP) from routes attached to listeners.
315	func buildL4Servers(logger logr.Logger, gateway *graph.Gateway, protocol v1.ProtocolType) []Layer4VirtualServer {
316		var servers []Layer4VirtualServer
317		protocolName := string(protocol)
318	
319		for _, l := range gateway.Listeners {
320			if !l.Valid || l.Source.Protocol != protocol {
321				continue
322			}
323	
324			for _, r := range l.L4Routes {
325				if !r.Valid {
326					continue
327				}
328	
329				backendRefs := r.Spec.GetBackendRefs()
330	
331				if len(backendRefs) == 0 {
332					logger.V(1).Info("Route has no valid backend references, skipping",
333						"route", r.Source.GetName(),
334						"protocol", protocolName,
335					)
336					continue
337				}
338	
339				var upstreams []Layer4Upstream
340				for _, br := range backendRefs {
341					if !br.Valid {
342						continue
343					}
344	
345					upstreamName := br.ServicePortReference()
346	
347					upstreams = append(upstreams, Layer4Upstream{
348						Name:   upstreamName,
349						Weight: br.Weight,
350					})
351				}
352	
353				if len(upstreams) == 0 {
354					logger.V(1).Info("No valid upstreams for route, skipping",
355						"route", r.Source.GetName(),
356						"protocol", protocolName,
357					)
358					continue
359				}
360	
361				server := Layer4VirtualServer{
362					Hostname:  "", // Layer4 doesn't use hostnames
363					Upstreams: upstreams,
364					Port:      l.Source.Port,
365				}
366	
367				servers = append(servers, server)
368			}
369		}
370	
371		// L4 routes are iterated from a map, so sort the resulting servers to produce a stable order.
372		// Preserve order so that this doesn't trigger an unnecessary reload.
373		sort.Slice(servers, func(i, j int) bool {
374			if servers[i].Port != servers[j].Port {
375				return servers[i].Port < servers[j].Port
376			}
377			if servers[i].Hostname != servers[j].Hostname {
378				return servers[i].Hostname < servers[j].Hostname
379			}
380			return layer4UpstreamsKey(servers[i].Upstreams) < layer4UpstreamsKey(servers[j].Upstreams)
381		})
382	
383		return servers
384	}
385	
386	// layer4UpstreamsKey builds a stable comparison key from a server's upstreams so that L4 servers
387	// sharing the same port and hostname are ordered deterministically.
388	func layer4UpstreamsKey(upstreams []Layer4Upstream) string {
389		names := make([]string, 0, len(upstreams))
390		for _, u := range upstreams {
391			names = append(names, u.Name)
392		}
393		return strings.Join(names, ",")
394	}
395	
396	// buildStreamUpstreams builds all stream upstreams.
397	func buildStreamUpstreams(
398		ctx context.Context,
399		logger logr.Logger,
400		gateway *graph.Gateway,
401		serviceResolver resolver.ServiceResolver,
402		referencedServices map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.ReferencedService,
403	) []Upstream {
404		// There can be duplicate upstreams if multiple routes reference the same upstream.
405		// We use a map to deduplicate them.
406		uniqueUpstreams := make(map[string]Upstream)
407	
408		gatewayNSName := client.ObjectKeyFromObject(gateway.Source)
409	
410		// Supported protocols for stream upstreams
411		supportedProtocols := map[v1.ProtocolType]bool{
412			v1.TLSProtocolType: true,
413			v1.TCPProtocolType: true,
414			v1.UDPProtocolType: true,
415		}
416	
417		for _, l := range gateway.Listeners {
418			if !l.Valid || !supportedProtocols[l.Source.Protocol] {
419				continue
420			}
421	
422			for _, route := range l.L4Routes {
423				if !route.Valid {
424					continue
425				}
426	
427				backendRefs := route.Spec.GetBackendRefs()
428				if len(backendRefs) == 0 {
429					continue
430				}
431	
432				// Process each backend reference
433				for _, br := range backendRefs {
434					if !br.Valid {
435						continue
436					}
437	
438					if _, ok := br.InvalidForGateways[gatewayNSName]; ok {
439						continue
440					}
441	
442					upstreamName := br.ServicePortReference()
443	
444					if _, exist := uniqueUpstreams[upstreamName]; exist {
445						continue
446					}
447	
448					var errMsg string
449	
450					// Use resolveUpstreamEndpoints to handle both regular and ExternalName services
451					eps, err := resolveUpstreamEndpoints(
452						ctx,
453						logger,
454						br,
455						serviceResolver,
456						referencedServices,
457					)
458					if err != nil {
459						errMsg = err.Error()
460					}
461	
462					uniqueUpstreams[upstreamName] = Upstream{
463						Name:      upstreamName,
464						Endpoints: eps,
465						ErrorMsg:  errMsg,
466					}
467				}
468			}
469		}
470	
471		if len(uniqueUpstreams) == 0 {
472			return nil
473		}
474	
475		upstreams := make([]Upstream, 0, len(uniqueUpstreams))
476	
477		for _, up := range uniqueUpstreams {
478			upstreams = append(upstreams, up)
479		}
480	
481		// Preserve order so that this doesn't trigger an unnecessary reload.
482		sort.Slice(upstreams, func(i, j int) bool {
483			return upstreams[i].Name < upstreams[j].Name
484		})
485	
486		return upstreams
487	}
488	
489	// buildSSLKeyPairs builds the SSLKeyPairs from the Secrets. It will only include Secrets that are referenced by
490	// valid gateway and its listeners, so that we don't include unused Secrets in the configuration of the data plane.
491	func buildSSLKeyPairs(
492		secretsMap map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
493		gateway *graph.Gateway,
494	) map[SSLKeyPairID]SSLKeyPair {
495		keyPairs := make(map[SSLKeyPairID]SSLKeyPair)
496	
497		for _, l := range gateway.Listeners {
498			if l.Valid && len(l.ResolvedSecrets) > 0 {
499				for _, secretNsName := range l.ResolvedSecrets {
500					id := generateSSLKeyPairID(secretNsName)
501					secret := secretsMap[secretNsName]
502					if secret != nil && secret.CertBundle != nil {
503						keyPairs[id] = SSLKeyPair{
504							Cert: secret.CertBundle.Cert.TLSCert,
505							Key:  secret.CertBundle.Cert.TLSPrivateKey,
506						}
507					}
508				}
509			}
510		}
511	
512		if gateway.Valid && gateway.SecretRef != nil {
513			id := generateSSLKeyPairID(*gateway.SecretRef)
514			secret := secretsMap[*gateway.SecretRef]
515			if secret != nil && secret.CertBundle != nil {
516				keyPairs[id] = SSLKeyPair{
517					Cert: secret.CertBundle.Cert.TLSCert,
518					Key:  secret.CertBundle.Cert.TLSPrivateKey,
519				}
520			}
521		}
522	
523		return keyPairs
524	}
525	
526	func buildJWTRemoteTLSCABundles(
527		authFilters map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.AuthenticationFilter,
528		secretsMap map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
529	) map[CertBundleID]CertBundle {
530		bundles := make(map[CertBundleID]CertBundle)
531	
532		for _, filter := range authFilters {
533			if !filter.Valid || filter.Source.Spec.JWT == nil {
534				continue
535			}
536	
537			specJWT := filter.Source.Spec.JWT
538			if specJWT.Source != ngfAPIv1alpha1.JWTKeySourceRemote {
539				continue
540			}
541			if specJWT.Remote == nil || len(specJWT.Remote.CACertificateRefs) == 0 {
542				continue
543			}
544	
545			for _, ref := range specJWT.Remote.CACertificateRefs {
546				secretNsName := types.NamespacedName{
547					Namespace: filter.Source.Namespace,
548					Name:      ref.Name,
549				}
550				secret := secretsMap[secretNsName]
551				if secret != nil && secret.Source != nil && secret.Source.Data[secrets.CAKey] != nil {
552					id := generateJWTRemoteTLSCABundleID(secretNsName.Namespace, secretNsName.Name)
553					bundles[id] = secret.Source.Data[secrets.CAKey]
554				}
555			}
556		}
557	
558		return bundles
559	}
560	
561	// listenerClientSettings captures the information about a listener
562	// for configuring SSL servers with client verification settings.
563	type listenerClientSettings struct {
564		CertBundleID   CertBundleID
565		validationMode v1.FrontendValidationModeType
566	}
567	
568	func buildFrontendTLSCertBundles(
569		gateway *graph.Gateway,
570		sslServers []VirtualServer,
571		refCertBundles []secrets.CertificateBundle,
572	) map[CertBundleID]CertBundle {
573		bundles := make(map[CertBundleID]CertBundle, len(refCertBundles))
574		clientSettingsMap := make(map[int32]listenerClientSettings)
575	
576		if !gateway.Valid || gateway.Source.Spec.TLS == nil || gateway.Source.Spec.TLS.Frontend == nil {
577			return bundles
578		}
579	
580		refCertBundleIndex := indexRefCertBundles(refCertBundles)
581	
582		for _, listener := range gateway.Listeners {
583			if listener.Source.Protocol != v1.HTTPSProtocolType {
584				continue
585			}
586	
587			if len(listener.CACertificateRefs) == 0 {
588				continue
589			}
590			// Create a unique cert bundle ID for this listener gateway combo.
591			// e.g. cert_bundle_default_gateway_443 for a HTTPS listener on port 443
592			// for a gateway in the default namespace.
593			caCertRef := types.NamespacedName{
594				Namespace: gateway.Source.Namespace,
595				Name: fmt.Sprintf("%s_%d",
596					gateway.Source.Name,
597					listener.Source.Port,
598				),
599			}
600			id := generateCertBundleID(caCertRef)
601			// We map listener port to the CertBundleID and ValidationMode of this listener
602			// to later configure the relevant SSL Servers with this data.
603			// This avoids iterating over each SSL Server for each Listener.
604			clientSettingsMap[listener.Source.Port] = listenerClientSettings{
605				CertBundleID:   id,
606				validationMode: listener.ValidationMode,
607			}
608			// If the validation mode is AllowInsecureFallback
609			// we do not want to configure any CA bundles for this listener.
610			if listener.ValidationMode != v1.AllowInsecureFallback {
611				bundles = getFrontendTLSCertBundles(
612					id,
613					bundles,
614					gateway,
615					refCertBundleIndex,
616					listener.CACertificateRefs,
617				)
618			}
619		}
620		addClientSettingsToSSLServers(sslServers, clientSettingsMap)
621		return bundles
622	}
623	
624	// refCertBundleKey is used as the key for indexing referenced certificate bundles
625	// when building frontend TLS cert bundles.
626	// It consists of the kind, namespace, and name of the referenced certificate bundle.
627	type refCertBundleKey struct {
628		kind      v1.Kind
629		namespace v1.Namespace
630		name      v1.ObjectName
631	}
632	
633	// indexRefCertBundles creates an index of the referenced certificate bundles
634	// based on their kind, namespace, and name for faster lookup when building frontend TLS cert bundles.
635	func indexRefCertBundles(
636		refCertBundles []secrets.CertificateBundle,
637	) map[refCertBundleKey]secrets.CertificateBundle {
638		index := make(map[refCertBundleKey]secrets.CertificateBundle, len(refCertBundles))
639		for _, bundle := range refCertBundles {
640			key := refCertBundleKey{
641				kind:      bundle.Kind,
642				namespace: v1.Namespace(bundle.Name.Namespace),
643				name:      v1.ObjectName(bundle.Name.Name),
644			}
645			index[key] = bundle
646		}
647		return index
648	}
649	
650	func getFrontendTLSCertBundles(
651		id CertBundleID,
652		bundles map[CertBundleID]CertBundle,
653		gateway *graph.Gateway,
654		refCertBundleIndex map[refCertBundleKey]secrets.CertificateBundle,
655		listenerCACertRefs []v1.ObjectReference,
656	) map[CertBundleID]CertBundle {
657		certBundles := make([]CertBundle, 0, len(listenerCACertRefs))
658		for _, ref := range listenerCACertRefs {
659			if ref.Name == "" {
660				continue
661			}
662			refNamespace := v1.Namespace(gateway.Source.Namespace)
663			if ref.Namespace != nil {
664				refNamespace = *ref.Namespace
665			}
666	
667			key := refCertBundleKey{
668				kind:      ref.Kind,
669				namespace: refNamespace,
670				name:      ref.Name,
671			}
672			if bundle, exists := refCertBundleIndex[key]; exists {
673				certRefData := getCertRefBundleData(bundle)
674				certBundles = append(certBundles, certRefData)
675			}
676		}
677		if len(certBundles) == 0 {
678			return bundles
679		}
680	
681		if _, exists := bundles[id]; !exists {
682			for _, v := range certBundles {
683				bundles[id] = append(bundles[id], v...)
684			}
685		}
686	
687		return bundles
688	}
689	
690	// addClientSettingsToSSLServers modifies existing SSL servers to assign
691	// client certificate verification settings based on the listener's validation mode and CA cert refs.
692	func addClientSettingsToSSLServers(
693		sslServers []VirtualServer,
694		clientSettingsMap map[int32]listenerClientSettings,
695	) {
696		for i := range sslServers {
697			if sslServers[i].SSL == nil {
698				continue
699			}
700			if clientSettings, exists := clientSettingsMap[sslServers[i].Port]; exists {
701				switch clientSettings.validationMode {
702				case v1.AllowInsecureFallback:
703					// Request client certificate but allow any certificate (valid, invalid, or none)
704					// Do not configure CA bundle verification for this mode
705					sslServers[i].SSL.ClientCertBundleID = ""
706					sslServers[i].SSL.VerifyClient = SSLVerifyClientOptionalNoCA
707					sslServers[i].SSL.RequireVerifiedCert = false
708				default:
709					// AllowValidOnly is default when no validation mode is specified.
710					sslServers[i].SSL.ClientCertBundleID = clientSettings.CertBundleID
711					sslServers[i].SSL.VerifyClient = SSLVerifyClientOn
712					sslServers[i].SSL.RequireVerifiedCert = true
713				}
714			}
715		}
716	}
717	
718	func buildRefCertificateBundles(
719		secretsMap map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
720		configMaps map[types.NamespacedName]*configmaps.CaCertConfigMap,
721	) []secrets.CertificateBundle {
722		bundles := []secrets.CertificateBundle{}
723	
724		for _, secret := range secretsMap {
725			if secret.CertBundle != nil {
726				bundles = append(bundles, *secret.CertBundle)
727			}
728		}
729	
730		for _, configMap := range configMaps {
731			if configMap.CertBundle != nil {
732				bundles = append(bundles, *configMap.CertBundle)
733			}
734		}
735	
736		return bundles
737	}
738	
739	func buildCertBundles(
740		refCertBundles []secrets.CertificateBundle,
741		backendGroups []BackendGroup,
742		tlsServers []Layer4VirtualServer,
743		extAuthCertBundleIDs map[CertBundleID]struct{},
744		authCertBundles map[CertBundleID]CertBundle,
745	) map[CertBundleID]CertBundle {
746		bundles := make(map[CertBundleID]CertBundle)
747	
748		maps.Copy(bundles, authCertBundles)
749	
750		referenced := make(map[CertBundleID]struct{}, len(extAuthCertBundleIDs))
751		for id := range extAuthCertBundleIDs {
752			referenced[id] = struct{}{}
753		}
754		for _, bg := range backendGroups {
755			for _, b := range bg.Backends {
756				if !b.Valid || b.VerifyTLS == nil {
757					continue
758				}
759				referenced[b.VerifyTLS.CertBundleID] = struct{}{}
760			}
761		}
762		for _, s := range tlsServers {
763			if s.VerifyTLS == nil {
764				continue
765			}
766			referenced[s.VerifyTLS.CertBundleID] = struct{}{}
767		}
768	
769		if len(referenced) == 0 {
770			return bundles
771		}
772	
773		for _, bundle := range refCertBundles {
774			id := generateCertBundleID(bundle.Name)
775			if _, exists := referenced[id]; exists {
776				bundles[id] = getCertRefBundleData(bundle)
777			}
778		}
779		return bundles
780	}
781	
782	func getCertRefBundleData(bundle secrets.CertificateBundle) []byte {
783		// the cert could be base64 encoded or plaintext
784		data := make([]byte, base64.StdEncoding.DecodedLen(len(bundle.Cert.CACert)))
785		n, err := base64.StdEncoding.Decode(data, bundle.Cert.CACert)
786		if err != nil {
787			data = bundle.Cert.CACert
788		} else {
789			data = data[:n]
790		}
791		return data
792	}
793	
794	func buildAuthSecrets(
795		authenticationFilters map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.AuthenticationFilter,
796		secretsMap map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
797	) map[AuthFileID]AuthFileData {
798		authFileData := make(map[AuthFileID]AuthFileData, len(authenticationFilters))
799	
800		for _, filter := range authenticationFilters {
801			if filter == nil || filter.Source == nil {
802				continue
803			}
804	
805			id, data := getAuthFileIDAndData(filter, secretsMap)
806	
807			if id == "" || data == nil {
808				continue
809			}
810	
811			authFileData[id] = data
812		}
813	
814		return authFileData
815	}
816	
817	func getAuthFileIDAndData(
818		filter *graph.AuthenticationFilter,
819		secretsMap map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
820	) (authFileID AuthFileID, data []byte) {
821		secretNsName := types.NamespacedName{
822			Namespace: filter.Source.Namespace,
823		}
824	
825		switch filter.Source.Spec.Type {
826		case ngfAPIv1alpha1.AuthTypeBasic:
827			if filter.Source.Spec.Basic == nil {
828				return "", nil
829			}
830			secretNsName.Name = filter.Source.Spec.Basic.SecretRef.Name
831			authFileID = GenerateAuthBasicFileID(secretNsName.Namespace, secretNsName.Name)
832		case ngfAPIv1alpha1.AuthTypeJWT:
833			if filter.Source.Spec.JWT == nil || filter.Source.Spec.JWT.File == nil {
834				return "", nil
835			}
836			secretNsName.Name = filter.Source.Spec.JWT.File.SecretRef.Name
837			authFileID = GenerateAuthJWTFileID(secretNsName.Namespace, secretNsName.Name)
838		}
839	
840		secret := secretsMap[secretNsName]
841		if secret == nil || secret.Source == nil {
842			return "", nil
843		}
844		data, exists := secret.Source.Data[secrets.AuthKey]
845		if !exists {
846			return "", nil
847		}
848	
849		return authFileID, data
850	}
851	
852	// buildAuthZConfigs builds AuthZConfig entries from authentication filters.
853	func buildAuthZConfigs(
854		authenticationFilters map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.AuthenticationFilter,
855	) []*AuthZConfig {
856		var authZConfigs []*AuthZConfig
857	
858		for nsName, filter := range authenticationFilters {
859			if filter == nil || filter.Source == nil || !filter.Valid || !filter.Referenced {
860				continue
861			}
862	
863			var authzSpec *ngfAPIv1alpha1.Authorization
864	
865			switch filter.Source.Spec.Type {
866			case ngfAPIv1alpha1.AuthTypeJWT:
867				if filter.Source.Spec.JWT != nil {
868					authzSpec = filter.Source.Spec.JWT.Authorization
869				}
870			case ngfAPIv1alpha1.AuthTypeOIDC:
871				if filter.Source.Spec.OIDC != nil {
872					authzSpec = filter.Source.Spec.OIDC.Authorization
873				}
874			}
875	
876			if authzSpec == nil {
877				continue
878			}
879	
880			filterNsName := strings.Join([]string{nsName.Namespace, nsName.Name}, "_")
881			filterPrefix := sanitizeVariablePrefix(filterNsName)
882			if cfg := buildAuthZConfigFromAuthZSpec(filterPrefix, authzSpec); cfg != nil {
883				cfg.FilterNsName = filterNsName
884				authZConfigs = append(authZConfigs, cfg)
885			}
886		}
887		return authZConfigs
888	}
889	
890	// buildAuthZConfigFromAuthZSpec builds a complete AuthZConfig from an Authorization spec.
891	// This produces:
892	//   - ClaimSets: auth_jwt_claim_set directives for each unique claim name
893	//   - RuleMaps: per-rule NGINX maps that validate claim values
894	//   - AuthZMap: a final aggregation map combining all rule results
895	//   - RequireVariable: the variable name for the auth_jwt_require directive
896	//   - ProxySetHeaders: claim-based proxy_set_header directives
897	func buildAuthZConfigFromAuthZSpec(
898		filterPrefix string,
899		authZSpec *ngfAPIv1alpha1.Authorization,
900	) *AuthZConfig {
901		if authZSpec == nil || len(authZSpec.Rules) == 0 {
902			return nil
903		}
904	
905		config := &AuthZConfig{}
906	
907		// 1. Build ClaimSets from unique claim names across all rules
908		config.AuthClaimSets = make(map[string][]string)
909		for _, rule := range authZSpec.Rules {
910			for _, claim := range rule.Claims {
911				varName := generateClaimVariableName(filterPrefix, claim.Name)
912				if _, exists := config.AuthClaimSets[varName]; !exists {
913					config.AuthClaimSets[varName] = splitClaimName(claim.Name)
914				}
915				// Get proxy_set_header entries
916				if claim.ProxySetHeader != nil && *claim.ProxySetHeader != "" {
917					config.ProxySetHeaders = append(config.ProxySetHeaders, HTTPHeader{
918						Name:  *claim.ProxySetHeader,
919						Value: varName,
920					})
921				}
922			}
923		}
924	
925		// 2. Build per-rule maps
926		ruleResultVars := make([]string, 0, len(authZSpec.Rules))
927		for ruleIdx, rule := range authZSpec.Rules {
928			requireType := ngfAPIv1alpha1.RequireTypeAny
929			if rule.Require != nil {
930				requireType = *rule.Require
931			}
932	
933			ruleMap := buildAuthZRuleMap(filterPrefix, ruleIdx, requireType, rule.Claims)
934			config.RuleMaps = append(config.RuleMaps, ruleMap)
935	
936			// Collect the result variable name for this map
937			ruleResultVars = append(ruleResultVars, ruleMap.Maps[len(ruleMap.Maps)-1].Variable)
938		}
939	
940		// 3. Build aggregation map
941		authZRequire := ngfAPIv1alpha1.RequireTypeAny
942		if authZSpec.Require != nil {
943			authZRequire = *authZSpec.Require
944		}
945		config.AuthZMap = buildAuthZRuleResultMap(filterPrefix, authZRequire, ruleResultVars)
946		if config.AuthZMap != nil {
947			config.RequireVariable = config.AuthZMap.Variable
948		} else {
949			// Single rule: use the rule's result variable directly
950			config.RequireVariable = ruleResultVars[len(ruleResultVars)-1]
951		}
952	
953		return config
954	}
955	
956	// buildAuthZRuleMap builds the NGINX maps for a single authorization rule.
957	func buildAuthZRuleMap(
958		filterPrefix string,
959		ruleIndex int,
960		requireType ngfAPIv1alpha1.RequireType,
961		claims []ngfAPIv1alpha1.Claim,
962	) AuthZRuleMap {
963		switch requireType {
964		case ngfAPIv1alpha1.RequireTypeAll:
965			return buildAuthZRuleMapAll(filterPrefix, ruleIndex, claims)
966		default: // RequireTypeAny
967			return buildAuthZRuleMapAny(filterPrefix, ruleIndex, claims)
968		}
969	}
970	
971	// buildAuthZRuleMapAny builds maps for require:Any mode.
972	// In Any mode, we create one map per claim, then a combining map.
973	// Example:
974	//
975	//	map $claim_iss $iss_rule_0 {
976	//	    ~^(?:.*\b)?val1(?:\b.*)?$ 1;
977	//	    default 0;
978	//	}
979	//	map $claim_aud $aud_rule_0 {
980	//	    ~^(?:.*\b)?val2(?:\b.*)?$ 1;
981	//	    default 0;
982	//	}
983	//	map $iss_rule_0$aud_rule_0 $rule_0_any {
984	//	    ~1 1;
985	//	    default 0;
986	//	}
987	func buildAuthZRuleMapAny(filterPrefix string, ruleIndex int, claims []ngfAPIv1alpha1.Claim) AuthZRuleMap {
988		var ruleMaps []shared.Map
989		perClaimVars := make([]string, 0, len(claims))
990	
991		for _, claim := range claims {
992			claimVarName := generateClaimVariableName(filterPrefix, claim.Name)
993			claimShortName := strings.TrimPrefix(claimVarName, "$"+filterPrefix+"_claim_")
994			// Use filterPrefix at start to scope per-filter.
995			perClaimVar := fmt.Sprintf("$%s_claim_%s_rule_%d", filterPrefix, claimShortName, ruleIndex)
996			perClaimVars = append(perClaimVars, perClaimVar)
997	
998			pattern := "\"~" + generateClaimValuePattern(claim.Values, &claim.Match, anyAnchors) + "\""
999	
1000			ruleMaps = append(ruleMaps, shared.Map{
1001				Source:   claimVarName,
1002				Variable: perClaimVar,
1003				Parameters: []shared.MapParameter{
1004					{Value: pattern, Result: "1"},
1005					{Value: "default", Result: "0"},
1006				},
1007			})
1008		}
1009	
1010		// Combining map: used only for rules[].claims in Any mode;
1011		// if any per-claim variable is 1, the rule passes
1012		resultVar := fmt.Sprintf("$%s_rule_%d_any", filterPrefix, ruleIndex)
1013		combiningSource := strings.Join(perClaimVars, "")
1014	
1015		ruleMaps = append(ruleMaps, shared.Map{
1016			Source:   combiningSource,
1017			Variable: resultVar,
1018			Parameters: []shared.MapParameter{
1019				{Value: "~1", Result: "1"},
1020				{Value: "default", Result: "0"},
1021			},
1022		})
1023	
1024		return AuthZRuleMap{Maps: ruleMaps, Require: ngfAPIv1alpha1.RequireTypeAny}
1025	}
1026	
1027	// buildAuthZRuleMapAll builds maps for require:All mode.
1028	// In All mode, all claim variables are concatenated as the map source,
1029	// and all values are matched simultaneously in a single regex pattern.
1030	// Example:
1031	//
1032	//	map $claim_iss+$claim_aud $rule_0_all {
1033	//	    ~^(?:.*\b)?myissuer(?:\b.*)?\+(?:.*\b)?myaudience(?:\b.*)?$ 1;
1034	//	    default 0;
1035	//	}
1036	func buildAuthZRuleMapAll(filterPrefix string, ruleIndex int, claims []ngfAPIv1alpha1.Claim) AuthZRuleMap {
1037		sources := make([]string, 0, len(claims))
1038		patterns := make([]string, 0, len(claims))
1039	
1040		for _, claim := range claims {
1041			sources = append(sources, generateClaimVariableName(filterPrefix, claim.Name))
1042			patterns = append(patterns, generateClaimValuePattern(claim.Values, &claim.Match, allAnchors))
1043		}
1044	
1045		resultVar := fmt.Sprintf("$%s_rule_%d_all", filterPrefix, ruleIndex)
1046		source := strings.Join(sources, "+")
1047		combinedPattern := "\"~^" + strings.Join(patterns, `\+`) + "$\""
1048	
1049		authZMap := shared.Map{
1050			Source:   source,
1051			Variable: resultVar,
1052			Parameters: []shared.MapParameter{
1053				{Value: combinedPattern, Result: "1"},
1054				{Value: "default", Result: "0"},
1055			},
1056		}
1057	
1058		return AuthZRuleMap{Maps: []shared.Map{authZMap}, Require: ngfAPIv1alpha1.RequireTypeAll}
1059	}
1060	
1061	// buildAuthZRuleResultMap builds the final aggregation map that combines all per-rule result
1062	// variables into a single NGINX map whose output variable is used by the auth_jwt_require directive.
1063	// Returns nil when there are zero or one rules.
1064	// When one rule exists, use the result variable directly.
1065	//
1066	// In require:Any mode, the concatenated rule results are checked for the presence of at least
1067	// one "1" i.e. at least one rule passed.
1068	// Example with 3 rules:
1069	//
1070	//	map $rule_0_any$rule_1_all$rule_2_any $test_auth_authz_require_any {
1071	//	    ~1      1;
1072	//	    default 0;
1073	//	}
1074	//
1075	// In require:All mode, the concatenated rule results must all be "1".
1076	// The match pattern is an exact string of N ones, where N is the number of rules.
1077	// Example with 3 rules:
1078	//
1079	//	map $rule_0_any$rule_1_all$rule_2_any $test_auth_authz_require_all {
1080	//	    111     1;
1081	//	    default 0;
1082	//	}
1083	func buildAuthZRuleResultMap(
1084		filterPrefix string,
1085		requireType ngfAPIv1alpha1.RequireType,
1086		ruleResultVars []string,
1087	) *AuthZMap {
1088		if len(ruleResultVars) == 0 {
1089			return nil
1090		}
1091	
1092		// If there's only one rule, no aggregation map is needed;
1093		// the caller uses the rule's result variable directly.
1094		if len(ruleResultVars) == 1 {
1095			return nil
1096		}
1097	
1098		var variable string
1099		var matchPattern string
1100		source := strings.Join(ruleResultVars, "")
1101	
1102		switch requireType {
1103		case ngfAPIv1alpha1.RequireTypeAny:
1104			variable = fmt.Sprintf("$%s_authz_require_any", filterPrefix)
1105			matchPattern = "~1" // if any rule result contains 1
1106		default: // RequireTypeAll
1107			variable = fmt.Sprintf("$%s_authz_require_all", filterPrefix)
1108			// All rule results must be 1: e.g., for 3 rules, match "111"
1109			matchPattern = strings.Repeat("1", len(ruleResultVars))
1110		}
1111	
1112		return &AuthZMap{
1113			Map: shared.Map{
1114				Source:   source,
1115				Variable: variable,
1116				Parameters: []shared.MapParameter{
1117					{Value: matchPattern, Result: "1"},
1118					{Value: "default", Result: "0"},
1119				},
1120			},
1121			Require: requireType,
1122		}
1123	}
1124	
1125	// generateClaimVariableName generates the NGINX variable name for a claim, scoped by filter prefix.
1126	// Dots, slashes, and dashes in claim names are replaced with underscores for NGINX variable compatibility,
1127	// since NGINX variable names only allow [a-zA-Z0-9_].
1128	// The filterPrefix ensures uniqueness across multiple AuthenticationFilters.
1129	// Examples (with filterPrefix "test_auth"):
1130	//   - "realm_access/roles" becomes "$test_auth_claim_realm_access_roles"
1131	//   - "app_1-role" becomes "$test_auth_claim_app_1_role"
1132	func generateClaimVariableName(filterPrefix, claimName string) string {
1133		safeName := sanitizeVariablePrefix(claimName)
1134		return fmt.Sprintf("$%s_claim_%s", filterPrefix, safeName)
1135	}
1136	
1137	// sanitizeVariablePrefix converts a string value into a valid NGINX variable prefix.
1138	// NGINX variable names only allow [a-zA-Z0-9_], so any other characters (e.g., dashes) are replaced
1139	// with underscores.
1140	func sanitizeVariablePrefix(value string) string {
1141		return strings.NewReplacer("-", "_", ".", "_", "/", "_").Replace(value)
1142	}
1143	
1144	// splitClaimName splits a claim name into parts for the auth_jwt_claim_set directive.
1145	// The "/" character is used as the nesting separator for nested JWT claims.
1146	// For example, "realm_access/roles" becomes ["realm_access", "roles"].
1147	// A claim name without "/" (e.g., "aud") returns ["aud"].
1148	func splitClaimName(name string) []string {
1149		return strings.Split(name, "/")
1150	}
1151	
1152	// claimPatternAnchors defines the regex prefix and suffix for wrapping claim value patterns.
1153	type claimPatternAnchors struct {
1154		prefix string
1155		suffix string
1156	}
1157	
1158	var (
1159		// allAnchors uses (?:.*,)? and (?:,.*)? to match a value within a comma-separated list
1160		// when multiple claims are concatenated with +.
1161		// Example: (?:.*,)?acme-co(?:,.*)? as part of a larger ^...$ anchored pattern.
1162		allAnchors = claimPatternAnchors{prefix: "(?:.*,)?", suffix: "(?:,.*)?"}
1163	
1164		// anyAnchors uses (?:^|,) and (?:,|$) to match a value within a comma-separated list
1165		// when the claim is evaluated independently.
1166		// Example: (?:^|,)cli(?:,|$) matches "cli", "cli,api", "api,cli", "api,cli,ops".
1167		anyAnchors = claimPatternAnchors{prefix: "(?:^|,)", suffix: "(?:,|$)"}
1168	)
1169	
1170	// generateClaimValuePattern generates a regex pattern for claim value matching,
1171	// wrapping the value pattern with the provided anchors.
1172	func generateClaimValuePattern(
1173		values []string,
1174		matchType *ngfAPIv1alpha1.ClaimMatchType,
1175		anchors claimPatternAnchors,
1176	) string {
1177		if len(values) == 0 {
1178			return ".*"
1179		}
1180	
1181		valuePattern := buildMapValuePattern(values, matchType)
1182	
1183		return fmt.Sprintf("%s%s%s", anchors.prefix, valuePattern, anchors.suffix)
1184	}
1185	
1186	// buildMapValuePattern builds a regex safe pattern for list of values
1187	// and applying regex escaping where necessary.
1188	// Examples:
1189	// - Values ["val1", "val2"] and exact match, returns (?:^|,)(?:val1|val2)(?:,|$)
1190	// - Value "https://issuer.example-1.com" with exact match returns https://issuer\.example-1\.com
1191	func buildMapValuePattern(values []string, matchType *ngfAPIv1alpha1.ClaimMatchType) string {
1192		patterns := make([]string, 0, len(values))
1193		for _, value := range values {
1194			if matchType != nil && *matchType == ngfAPIv1alpha1.ClaimMatchTypeRegex {
1195				// Regex match: use the value as-is
1196				patterns = append(patterns, value)
1197			} else {
1198				// Exact match: escape regex special characters
1199				patterns = append(patterns, regexEscape(value))
1200			}
1201		}
1202	
1203		if len(patterns) == 1 {
1204			return patterns[0]
1205		}
1206		return generateListMatchPattern(patterns)
1207	}
1208	
1209	// generateListMatchPattern generates a regex pattern that matches any of the provided patterns.
1210	// Example: input ["val1", "val2"] returns (val1|val2).
1211	func generateListMatchPattern(patterns []string) string {
1212		return "(" + strings.Join(patterns, "|") + ")"
1213	}
1214	
1215	// regexEscape escapes special regex characters in a string.
1216	// Example: "https://issuer.example-1.com" becomes "https://issuer\.example-1\.com"
1217	func regexEscape(value string) string {
1218		special := `\.+*?^${}()|[]`
1219		var result strings.Builder
1220		for _, character := range value {
1221			if strings.ContainsRune(special, character) {
1222				result.WriteRune('\\')
1223			}
1224			result.WriteRune(character)
1225		}
1226		return result.String()
1227	}
1228	
1229	func buildBackendGroups(servers []VirtualServer) []BackendGroup {
1230		type key struct {
1231			nsname      types.NamespacedName
1232			ruleIdx     int
1233			pathRuleIdx int
1234		}
1235	
1236		// There can be duplicate backend groups if a route is attached to multiple listeners.
1237		// We use a map to deduplicate them.
1238		uniqueGroups := make(map[key]BackendGroup)
1239	
1240		for _, s := range servers {
1241			for _, pr := range s.PathRules {
1242				for _, mr := range pr.MatchRules {
1243					group := mr.BackendGroup
1244	
1245					k := key{
1246						nsname:      group.Source,
1247						ruleIdx:     group.RuleIdx,
1248						pathRuleIdx: group.PathRuleIdx,
1249					}
1250	
1251					uniqueGroups[k] = group
1252				}
1253			}
1254		}
1255	
1256		if len(uniqueGroups) == 0 {
1257			return nil
1258		}
1259	
1260		groups := make([]BackendGroup, 0, len(uniqueGroups))
1261		for _, group := range uniqueGroups {
1262			groups = append(groups, group)
1263		}
1264	
1265		return groups
1266	}
1267	
1268	func newBackendGroup(
1269		refs []graph.BackendRef,
1270		gatewayName types.NamespacedName,
1271		sourceNsName types.NamespacedName,
1272		ruleIdx int,
1273		referencedServices map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.ReferencedService,
1274	) (BackendGroup, bool) {
1275		var backends []Backend
1276	
1277		if len(refs) > 0 {
1278			backends = make([]Backend, 0, len(refs))
1279		}
1280		var inferencePoolBackendExists bool
1281	
1282		for _, ref := range refs {
1283			if ref.IsMirrorBackend || ref.IsExternalAuthBackend {
1284				continue
1285			}
1286	
1287			valid := ref.Valid
1288			if _, ok := ref.InvalidForGateways[gatewayName]; ok {
1289				valid = false
1290			}
1291	
1292			inferencePoolBackendExists = inferencePoolBackendExists || ref.IsInferencePool
1293	
1294			var eppRef *EndpointPickerConfig
1295			if ref.EndpointPickerConfig.EndpointPickerRef != nil {
1296				eppRef = &EndpointPickerConfig{
1297					EndpointPickerRef: ref.EndpointPickerConfig.EndpointPickerRef,
1298					NsName:            ref.EndpointPickerConfig.NsName,
1299				}
1300			}
1301	
1302			// Check if this backend is an ExternalName service
1303			externalHostname := getExternalHostname(ref.SvcNsName, referencedServices)
1304	
1305			var appProtocol string
1306			if ref.ServicePort.AppProtocol != nil {
1307				appProtocol = *ref.ServicePort.AppProtocol
1308			}
1309	
1310			backends = append(backends, Backend{
1311				UpstreamName:         ref.ServicePortReference(),
1312				Weight:               ref.Weight,
1313				Valid:                valid,
1314				VerifyTLS:            convertBackendTLS(ref.BackendTLSPolicy, gatewayName),
1315				EndpointPickerConfig: eppRef,
1316				ExternalHostname:     externalHostname,
1317				AppProtocol:          appProtocol,
1318			})
1319		}
1320	
1321		return BackendGroup{
1322			Backends: backends,
1323			Source:   sourceNsName,
1324			RuleIdx:  ruleIdx,
1325		}, inferencePoolBackendExists
1326	}
1327	
1328	func convertBackendTLS(btp *graph.BackendTLSPolicy, gwNsName types.NamespacedName) *VerifyTLS {
1329		if btp == nil || !btp.Valid {
1330			return nil
1331		}
1332	
1333		if !slices.Contains(btp.Gateways, gwNsName) {
1334			return nil
1335		}
1336	
1337		verify := &VerifyTLS{}
1338		if btp.CaCertRef.Name != "" {
1339			verify.CertBundleID = generateCertBundleID(btp.CaCertRef)
1340		} else {
1341			verify.RootCAPath = AlpineSSLRootCAPath
1342		}
1343		verify.Hostname = string(btp.Source.Spec.Validation.Hostname)
1344		return verify
1345	}
1346	
1347	func buildServers(
1348		gateway *graph.Gateway,
1349		referencedServices map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.ReferencedService,
1350		referencedSecrets map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
1351	) (http, ssl []VirtualServer, sslListenerHostnames map[int32][]string, extAuthCertBundleIDs map[CertBundleID]struct{}) {
1352		rulesForProtocol := map[v1.ProtocolType]portPathRules{
1353			v1.HTTPProtocolType:  make(portPathRules),
1354			v1.HTTPSProtocolType: make(portPathRules),
1355		}
1356		extAuthCertBundleIDs = make(map[CertBundleID]struct{})
1357	
1358		for _, l := range gateway.Listeners {
1359			if l.Source.Protocol == v1.TLSProtocolType ||
1360				l.Source.Protocol == v1.TCPProtocolType ||
1361				l.Source.Protocol == v1.UDPProtocolType {
1362				continue
1363			}
1364			if l.Valid {
1365				rules := rulesForProtocol[l.Source.Protocol][l.Source.Port]
1366				if rules == nil {
1367					rules = newHostPathRules()
1368					rulesForProtocol[l.Source.Protocol][l.Source.Port] = rules
1369				}
1370	
1371				rules.upsertListener(l, gateway, referencedServices, referencedSecrets, extAuthCertBundleIDs)
1372	
1373				if l.Source.Protocol == v1.HTTPSProtocolType {
1374					hostname := ""
1375					if l.Source.Hostname != nil {
1376						hostname = string(*l.Source.Hostname)
1377					}
1378					if sslListenerHostnames == nil {
1379						sslListenerHostnames = make(map[int32][]string)
1380					}
1381					sslListenerHostnames[l.Source.Port] = append(sslListenerHostnames[l.Source.Port], hostname)
1382				}
1383			}
1384		}
1385	
1386		httpRules := rulesForProtocol[v1.HTTPProtocolType]
1387		sslRules := rulesForProtocol[v1.HTTPSProtocolType]
1388	
1389		httpServers, sslServers := httpRules.buildServers(), sslRules.buildServers()
1390	
1391		pols := buildPolicies(gateway, gateway.Policies)
1392	
1393		for i := range httpServers {
1394			httpServers[i].Policies = pols
1395		}
1396	
1397		for i := range sslServers {
1398			sslServers[i].Policies = pols
1399		}
1400	
1401		return httpServers, sslServers, sslListenerHostnames, extAuthCertBundleIDs
1402	}
1403	
1404	// portPathRules keeps track of hostPathRules per port.
1405	type portPathRules map[v1.PortNumber]*hostPathRules
1406	
1407	func (p portPathRules) buildServers() []VirtualServer {
1408		serverCount := 0
1409		for _, rules := range p {
1410			serverCount += rules.maxServerCount()
1411		}
1412	
1413		servers := make([]VirtualServer, 0, serverCount)
1414	
1415		for _, rules := range p {
1416			servers = append(servers, rules.buildServers()...)
1417		}
1418	
1419		return servers
1420	}
1421	
1422	type pathAndType struct {
1423		path     string
1424		pathType v1.PathMatchType
1425	}
1426	
1427	type hostPathRules struct {
1428		rulesPerHost     map[string]map[pathAndType]PathRule
1429		listenersForHost map[string]*graph.Listener
1430		httpsListeners   []*graph.Listener
1431		port             int32
1432		listenersExist   bool
1433	}
1434	
1435	func newHostPathRules() *hostPathRules {
1436		return &hostPathRules{
1437			rulesPerHost:     make(map[string]map[pathAndType]PathRule),
1438			listenersForHost: make(map[string]*graph.Listener),
1439			httpsListeners:   make([]*graph.Listener, 0),
1440		}
1441	}
1442	
1443	func (hpr *hostPathRules) upsertListener(
1444		l *graph.Listener,
1445		gateway *graph.Gateway,
1446		referencedServices map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.ReferencedService,
1447		referencedSecrets map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
1448		extAuthCertBundleIDs map[CertBundleID]struct{},
1449	) {
1450		hpr.listenersExist = true
1451		hpr.port = l.Source.Port
1452	
1453		if l.Source.Protocol == v1.HTTPSProtocolType {
1454			hpr.httpsListeners = append(hpr.httpsListeners, l)
1455		}
1456	
1457		for _, r := range l.Routes {
1458			if !r.Valid {
1459				continue
1460			}
1461	
1462			hpr.upsertRoute(r, l, gateway, referencedServices, referencedSecrets, extAuthCertBundleIDs)
1463		}
1464	}
1465	
1466	func (hpr *hostPathRules) upsertRoute(
1467		route *graph.L7Route,
1468		listener *graph.Listener,
1469		gateway *graph.Gateway,
1470		referencedServices map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.ReferencedService,
1471		referencedSecrets map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
1472		extAuthCertBundleIDs map[CertBundleID]struct{},
1473	) {
1474		var hostnames []string
1475		GRPC := route.RouteType == graph.RouteTypeGRPC
1476	
1477		var objectSrc *metav1.ObjectMeta
1478	
1479		routeNsName := client.ObjectKeyFromObject(route.Source)
1480	
1481		if GRPC {
1482			objectSrc = &helpers.MustCastObject[*v1.GRPCRoute](route.Source).ObjectMeta
1483		} else {
1484			objectSrc = &helpers.MustCastObject[*v1.HTTPRoute](route.Source).ObjectMeta
1485		}
1486	
1487		for _, p := range route.ParentRefs {
1488			if val, exist := p.Attachment.AcceptedHostnames[graph.CreateParentRefListenerKeyFromListener(listener)]; exist {
1489				hostnames = val
1490				break
1491			}
1492		}
1493	
1494		for _, h := range hostnames {
1495			if prevListener, exists := hpr.listenersForHost[h]; exists {
1496				// override the previous listener if the new one has a more specific hostname
1497				if listenerHostnameMoreSpecific(listener.Source.Hostname, prevListener.Source.Hostname) {
1498					hpr.listenersForHost[h] = listener
1499				}
1500			} else {
1501				hpr.listenersForHost[h] = listener
1502			}
1503	
1504			if _, exist := hpr.rulesPerHost[h]; !exist {
1505				hpr.rulesPerHost[h] = make(map[pathAndType]PathRule)
1506			}
1507		}
1508	
1509		for idx, rule := range route.Spec.Rules {
1510			if !rule.ValidMatches {
1511				continue
1512			}
1513	
1514			var filters HTTPFilters
1515			if rule.Filters.Valid {
1516				filters = createHTTPFilters(
1517					rule.Filters.Filters,
1518					idx,
1519					routeNsName,
1520					referencedSecrets,
1521					rule.BackendRefs,
1522					listener.GatewayName,
1523					extAuthCertBundleIDs,
1524				)
1525			} else {
1526				filters = HTTPFilters{
1527					InvalidFilter: &InvalidHTTPFilter{},
1528				}
1529			}
1530	
1531			pols := buildPolicies(gateway, route.Policies)
1532	
1533			for _, h := range hostnames {
1534				for _, m := range rule.Matches {
1535					path := getPath(m.Path)
1536	
1537					key := pathAndType{
1538						path:     path,
1539						pathType: *m.Path.Type,
1540					}
1541	
1542					hostRule, exist := hpr.rulesPerHost[h][key]
1543					if !exist {
1544						hostRule.Path = path
1545						hostRule.PathType = convertPathType(*m.Path.Type)
1546						hostRule.Policies = append(hostRule.Policies, pols...)
1547					}
1548	
1549					hostRule.GRPC = GRPC
1550					backendGroup, inferencePoolBackendExists := newBackendGroup(
1551						rule.BackendRefs,
1552						listener.GatewayName,
1553						routeNsName,
1554						idx,
1555						referencedServices,
1556					)
1557					if inferencePoolBackendExists {
1558						hostRule.HasInferenceBackends = true
1559					}
1560	
1561					hostRule.MatchRules = append(hostRule.MatchRules, MatchRule{
1562						Source:       objectSrc,
1563						BackendGroup: backendGroup,
1564						Filters:      filters,
1565						Match:        convertMatch(m),
1566					})
1567	
1568					hpr.rulesPerHost[h][key] = hostRule
1569				}
1570			}
1571		}
1572	}
1573	
1574	func (hpr *hostPathRules) buildServers() []VirtualServer {
1575		servers := make([]VirtualServer, 0, len(hpr.rulesPerHost)+len(hpr.httpsListeners))
1576	
1577		for h, rules := range hpr.rulesPerHost {
1578			s := VirtualServer{
1579				Hostname:  h,
1580				PathRules: make([]PathRule, 0, len(rules)),
1581				Port:      hpr.port,
1582			}
1583	
1584			l, ok := hpr.listenersForHost[h]
1585			if !ok {
1586				panic(fmt.Sprintf("no listener found for hostname: %s", h))
1587			}
1588	
1589			if len(l.ResolvedSecrets) > 0 {
1590				s.SSL = buildSSL(l)
1591			}
1592	
1593			for _, r := range rules {
1594				sortMatchRules(r.MatchRules)
1595	
1596				s.PathRules = append(s.PathRules, r)
1597			}
1598	
1599			sortPathRules(s.PathRules)
1600	
1601			for pathRuleIdx := range s.PathRules {
1602				for matchRuleIdx := range s.PathRules[pathRuleIdx].MatchRules {
1603					s.PathRules[pathRuleIdx].MatchRules[matchRuleIdx].BackendGroup.PathRuleIdx = pathRuleIdx
1604				}
1605			}
1606	
1607			servers = append(servers, s)
1608		}
1609	
1610		var defaultSSL *SSL
1611		for _, l := range hpr.httpsListeners {
1612			hostname := getListenerHostname(l.Source.Hostname)
1613			// Generate a 404 ssl server block for listeners with no routes or listeners with wildcard (match-all) routes.
1614			// If SNI isn't set in a request, the default ssl server will be used first to terminate TLS,
1615			// then the Host header will be used to route to the correct server block.
1616			// If there is no matching hostname, then this wildcard server will be used.
1617			if len(l.Routes) == 0 || hostname == wildcardHostname {
1618				s := VirtualServer{
1619					Hostname: hostname,
1620					Port:     hpr.port,
1621				}
1622	
1623				if len(l.ResolvedSecrets) > 0 {
1624					s.SSL = buildSSL(l)
1625	
1626					// If this is a wildcard, save SSL config for default server
1627					if hostname == wildcardHostname {
1628						defaultSSL = s.SSL
1629					}
1630				}
1631	
1632				servers = append(servers, s)
1633			}
1634		}
1635	
1636		// if any listeners exist, we need to generate a default server block.
1637		if hpr.listenersExist {
1638			vs := VirtualServer{
1639				IsDefault: true,
1640				Port:      hpr.port,
1641				SSL:       defaultSSL,
1642			}
1643	
1644			servers = append(servers, vs)
1645		}
1646	
1647		// We sort the servers so the order is preserved after reconfiguration.
1648		sort.Slice(servers, func(i, j int) bool {
1649			return servers[i].Hostname < servers[j].Hostname
1650		})
1651	
1652		return servers
1653	}
1654	
1655	func buildSSL(listener *graph.Listener) *SSL {
1656		keyPairIDs := make([]SSLKeyPairID, 0, len(listener.ResolvedSecrets))
1657		for _, secretNsName := range listener.ResolvedSecrets {
1658			keyPairIDs = append(keyPairIDs, generateSSLKeyPairID(secretNsName))
1659		}
1660	
1661		ssl := &SSL{
1662			KeyPairIDs: keyPairIDs,
1663		}
1664	
1665		if listener.Source.TLS != nil && listener.Source.TLS.Options != nil {
1666			if protocols, ok := listener.Source.TLS.Options[graph.SSLProtocolsKey]; ok {
1667				ssl.Protocols = string(protocols)
1668			}
1669			if ciphers, ok := listener.Source.TLS.Options[graph.SSLCiphersKey]; ok {
1670				ssl.Ciphers = string(ciphers)
1671			}
1672			if prefer, ok := listener.Source.TLS.Options[graph.SSLPreferServerCiphersKey]; ok {
1673				ssl.PreferServerCiphers = (string(prefer) == "on")
1674			}
1675		}
1676	
1677		return ssl
1678	}
1679	
1680	// maxServerCount returns the maximum number of VirtualServers that can be built from the host path rules.
1681	func (hpr *hostPathRules) maxServerCount() int {
1682		// to calculate max # of servers we add up:
1683		// - # of hostnames
1684		// - # of https listeners - this is to account for https wildcard default servers
1685		// - default server - for every hostPathRules we generate 1 default server
1686		return len(hpr.rulesPerHost) + len(hpr.httpsListeners) + 1
1687	}
1688	
1689	func buildUpstreams(
1690		ctx context.Context,
1691		logger logr.Logger,
1692		gateway *graph.Gateway,
1693		svcResolver resolver.ServiceResolver,
1694		referencedServices map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.ReferencedService,
1695	) []Upstream {
1696		// There can be duplicate upstreams if multiple routes reference the same upstream.
1697		// We use a map to deduplicate them.
1698		uniqueUpstreams := make(map[string]Upstream)
1699	
1700		for _, l := range gateway.Listeners {
1701			if !l.Valid {
1702				continue
1703			}
1704	
1705			for _, route := range l.Routes {
1706				if !route.Valid {
1707					continue
1708				}
1709	
1710				for _, rule := range route.Spec.Rules {
1711					if !rule.ValidMatches || !rule.Filters.Valid {
1712						// don't generate upstreams for rules that have invalid matches or filters
1713						continue
1714					}
1715	
1716					for _, br := range rule.BackendRefs {
1717						if upstream := buildUpstream(
1718							ctx,
1719							logger,
1720							br,
1721							gateway,
1722							svcResolver,
1723							referencedServices,
1724							uniqueUpstreams,
1725							br.SessionPersistence,
1726						); upstream != nil {
1727							uniqueUpstreams[upstream.Name] = *upstream
1728						}
1729					}
1730				}
1731			}
1732		}
1733	
1734		if len(uniqueUpstreams) == 0 {
1735			return nil
1736		}
1737	
1738		upstreams := make([]Upstream, 0, len(uniqueUpstreams))
1739	
1740		for _, up := range uniqueUpstreams {
1741			upstreams = append(upstreams, up)
1742		}
1743	
1744		// Preserve order so that this doesn't trigger an unnecessary reload.
1745		sort.Slice(upstreams, func(i, j int) bool {
1746			return upstreams[i].Name < upstreams[j].Name
1747		})
1748	
1749		return upstreams
1750	}
1751	
1752	func buildUpstream(
1753		ctx context.Context,
1754		logger logr.Logger,
1755		br graph.BackendRef,
1756		gateway *graph.Gateway,
1757		svcResolver resolver.ServiceResolver,
1758		referencedServices map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.ReferencedService,
1759		uniqueUpstreams map[string]Upstream,
1760		sessionPersistence *graph.SessionPersistenceConfig,
1761	) *Upstream {
1762		if !br.Valid {
1763			return nil
1764		}
1765	
1766		gatewayNSName := client.ObjectKeyFromObject(gateway.Source)
1767		if _, ok := br.InvalidForGateways[gatewayNSName]; ok {
1768			return nil
1769		}
1770	
1771		upstreamName := br.ServicePortReference()
1772		_, exist := uniqueUpstreams[upstreamName]
1773	
1774		if exist {
1775			return nil
1776		}
1777	
1778		var errMsg string
1779		eps, err := resolveUpstreamEndpoints(
1780			ctx,
1781			logger,
1782			br,
1783			svcResolver,
1784			referencedServices,
1785		)
1786		if err != nil {
1787			errMsg = err.Error()
1788			logger.V(1).Info("failed to resolve endpoints, endpoints may not be ready", "error", errMsg, "service", br.SvcNsName)
1789		} else {
1790			logger.V(1).Info("successfully resolved endpoints", "service", br.SvcNsName)
1791		}
1792	
1793		var upstreamPolicies []policies.Policy
1794		if graphSvc, exists := referencedServices[br.SvcNsName]; exists {
1795			upstreamPolicies = buildPolicies(gateway, graphSvc.Policies)
1796		}
1797	
1798		var sp SessionPersistenceConfig
1799		if sessionPersistence != nil {
1800			sp = SessionPersistenceConfig{
1801				Name:        sessionPersistence.Name,
1802				Expiry:      sessionPersistence.Expiry,
1803				Path:        sessionPersistence.Path,
1804				SessionType: CookieBasedSessionPersistence,
1805			}
1806		}
1807	
1808		return &Upstream{
1809			Name:               upstreamName,
1810			Endpoints:          eps,
1811			ErrorMsg:           errMsg,
1812			Policies:           upstreamPolicies,
1813			SessionPersistence: sp,
1814			StateFileKey:       br.BaseServicePortKey(),
1815		}
1816	}
1817	
1818	func getListenerHostname(h *v1.Hostname) string {
1819		if h == nil || *h == "" {
1820			return wildcardHostname
1821		}
1822	
1823		return string(*h)
1824	}
1825	
1826	func getPath(path *v1.HTTPPathMatch) string {
1827		if path == nil || path.Value == nil || *path.Value == "" {
1828			return "/"
1829		}
1830		return *path.Value
1831	}
1832	
1833	func createHTTPFilters(
1834		filters []graph.Filter,
1835		ruleIdx int,
1836		routeNsName types.NamespacedName,
1837		referencedSecrets map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
1838		backendRefs []graph.BackendRef,
1839		gwNsName types.NamespacedName,
1840		extAuthCertBundleIDs map[CertBundleID]struct{},
1841	) HTTPFilters {
1842		var result HTTPFilters
1843	
1844		for _, f := range filters {
1845			switch f.FilterType {
1846			case graph.FilterRequestRedirect:
1847				result.addRequestRedirect(f.RequestRedirect)
1848			case graph.FilterURLRewrite:
1849				result.addURLRewrite(f.URLRewrite)
1850			case graph.FilterRequestMirror:
1851				result.addRequestMirror(f.RequestMirror, ruleIdx, routeNsName)
1852			case graph.FilterRequestHeaderModifier:
1853				result.addRequestHeaderModifier(f.RequestHeaderModifier)
1854			case graph.FilterResponseHeaderModifier:
1855				result.addResponseHeaderModifier(f.ResponseHeaderModifier)
1856			case graph.FilterExtensionRef:
1857				result.addExtensionRef(f.ResolvedExtensionRef, referencedSecrets)
1858			case graph.FilterCORS:
1859				result.addCORS(f.CORS)
1860			case graph.FilterExternalAuth:
1861				result.addExternalAuth(f.ExternalAuth, backendRefs, routeNsName, ruleIdx, gwNsName, extAuthCertBundleIDs)
1862			}
1863		}
1864	
1865		return result
1866	}
1867	
1868	func (hf *HTTPFilters) addRequestRedirect(redirect *v1.HTTPRequestRedirectFilter) {
1869		if hf.RequestRedirect == nil {
1870			// using the first filter
1871			hf.RequestRedirect = convertHTTPRequestRedirectFilter(redirect)
1872		}
1873	}
1874	
1875	func (hf *HTTPFilters) addURLRewrite(rewrite *v1.HTTPURLRewriteFilter) {
1876		if hf.RequestURLRewrite == nil {
1877			// using the first filter
1878			hf.RequestURLRewrite = convertHTTPURLRewriteFilter(rewrite)
1879		}
1880	}
1881	
1882	func (hf *HTTPFilters) addRequestMirror(
1883		mirror *v1.HTTPRequestMirrorFilter,
1884		ruleIdx int,
1885		routeNsName types.NamespacedName,
1886	) {
1887		hf.RequestMirrors = append(
1888			hf.RequestMirrors,
1889			convertHTTPRequestMirrorFilter(mirror, ruleIdx, routeNsName),
1890		)
1891	}
1892	
1893	func (hf *HTTPFilters) addRequestHeaderModifier(modifier *v1.HTTPHeaderFilter) {
1894		if hf.RequestHeaderModifiers == nil {
1895			// using the first filter
1896			hf.RequestHeaderModifiers = convertHTTPHeaderFilter(modifier)
1897		}
1898	}
1899	
1900	func (hf *HTTPFilters) addResponseHeaderModifier(modifier *v1.HTTPHeaderFilter) {
1901		if hf.ResponseHeaderModifiers == nil {
1902			// using the first filter
1903			hf.ResponseHeaderModifiers = convertHTTPHeaderFilter(modifier)
1904		}
1905	}
1906	
1907	func (hf *HTTPFilters) addExtensionRef(
1908		ref *graph.ExtensionRefFilter,
1909		referencedSecrets map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
1910	) {
1911		if ref == nil {
1912			return
1913		}
1914	
1915		if ref.SnippetsFilter != nil {
1916			hf.SnippetsFilters = append(
1917				hf.SnippetsFilters,
1918				convertSnippetsFilter(ref.SnippetsFilter),
1919			)
1920		}
1921		if ref.AuthenticationFilter != nil {
1922			hf.AuthenticationFilter = convertAuthenticationFilter(
1923				ref.AuthenticationFilter,
1924				referencedSecrets,
1925			)
1926		}
1927	}
1928	
1929	func (hf *HTTPFilters) addCORS(cors *v1.HTTPCORSFilter) {
1930		if hf.CORSFilter == nil {
1931			hf.CORSFilter = convertHTTPCORSFilter(cors)
1932		}
1933	}
1934	
1935	func (hf *HTTPFilters) addExternalAuth(
1936		extAuth *v1.HTTPExternalAuthFilter,
1937		backendRefs []graph.BackendRef,
1938		routeNsName types.NamespacedName,
1939		ruleIdx int,
1940		gwNsName types.NamespacedName,
1941		extAuthCertBundleIDs map[CertBundleID]struct{},
1942	) {
1943		if hf.ExternalAuthFilter != nil {
1944			return
1945		}
1946	
1947		for _, br := range backendRefs {
1948			if br.IsExternalAuthBackend && br.Valid {
1949				hf.ExternalAuthFilter = convertHTTPExternalAuthFilter(extAuth, br, routeNsName, ruleIdx, gwNsName)
1950				if hf.ExternalAuthFilter.VerifyTLS != nil && extAuthCertBundleIDs != nil {
1951					extAuthCertBundleIDs[hf.ExternalAuthFilter.VerifyTLS.CertBundleID] = struct{}{}
1952				}
1953				break
1954			}
1955		}
1956	}

1958	// listenerHostnameMoreSpecific returns true if host1 is more specific than host2.
1959	func listenerHostnameMoreSpecific(host1, host2 *v1.Hostname) bool {
1960		var host1Str, host2Str string
1961		if host1 != nil {
1962			host1Str = string(*host1)
1963		}
1964	
1965		if host2 != nil {
1966			host2Str = string(*host2)
1967		}
1968	
1969		return graph.GetMoreSpecificHostname(host1Str, host2Str) == host1Str
1970	}

1972	// generateSSLKeyPairID generates an ID for the SSL key pair based on the Secret namespaced name.
1973	// It is guaranteed to be unique per unique namespaced name.
1974	// The ID is safe to use as a file name.
1975	func generateSSLKeyPairID(secret types.NamespacedName) SSLKeyPairID {
1976		return SSLKeyPairID(fmt.Sprintf("ssl_keypair_%s_%s", secret.Namespace, secret.Name))
1977	}

1979	// generateCertBundleID generates an ID for the certificate bundle based on the ConfigMap/Secret namespaced name.
1980	// It is guaranteed to be unique per unique namespaced name.
1981	// The ID is safe to use as a file name.
1982	func generateCertBundleID(caCertRef types.NamespacedName) CertBundleID {
1983		return CertBundleID(fmt.Sprintf("cert_bundle_%s_%s", caCertRef.Namespace, caCertRef.Name))
1984	}
1985	
1986	// generateCRLBundleID generates an ID for a CRL bundle.
1987	// Uses a distinct prefix to avoid collisions when the same secret is referenced as both CA cert and CRL.
1988	func generateCRLBundleID(ref types.NamespacedName) CertBundleID {
1989		return CertBundleID(fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s_%s", crlBundleIDPrefix, ref.Namespace, ref.Name))
1990	}
1991	
1992	// IsCRLBundle reports whether this CertBundleID identifies a CRL bundle.
1993	func (id CertBundleID) IsCRLBundle() bool {
1994		return strings.HasPrefix(string(id), crlBundleIDPrefix)
1995	}

1997	// generateJWTRemoteTLSCABundleID generates an ID for JWT remote TLS CA bundle based on the Secret namespaced name.
1998	// It is guaranteed to be unique per unique namespaced name.
1999	// The ID is safe to use as a file name.
2000	func generateJWTRemoteTLSCABundleID(namespace, secretName string) CertBundleID {
2001		return CertBundleID(fmt.Sprintf("jwt_remote_tls_ca_%s_%s", namespace, secretName))

2004	// GenerateAuthBasicFileID is used to generate IDs for basic auth files.

2009	// GenerateAuthJWTFileID is used to generate IDs for jwt auth files.

2014	// buildOIDCProviderFromAuthenticationFilters builds the OIDC provider configs from the processed
2015	// authentication filters. It also returns any certificate bundles (CA certs and CRLs) that are needed.
2016	func buildOIDCProviderFromAuthenticationFilters(
2017		authFilters map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.AuthenticationFilter,
2018		referencedSecrets map[types.NamespacedName]*secrets.Secret,
2019	) ([]OIDCProvider, map[CertBundleID]CertBundle) {
2020		var providers []OIDCProvider
2021		certBundles := make(map[CertBundleID]CertBundle)
2022	
2023		for _, af := range authFilters {
2024			if !af.Valid || !af.Referenced {
2025				continue
2026			}
2027			if af.Source.Spec.Type != ngfAPIv1alpha1.AuthTypeOIDC {
2028				continue
2029			}
2030			converted := convertAuthenticationFilter(af, referencedSecrets)
2031			if converted.OIDC == nil || converted.OIDC.Provider == nil {
2032				continue
2033			}
2034			provider := *converted.OIDC.Provider
2035			if provider.CACertBundleID != "" && provider.CACertData != nil {
2036				certBundles[provider.CACertBundleID] = provider.CACertData
2037			}
2038			if provider.CRLBundleID != "" && provider.CRLData != nil {
2039				certBundles[provider.CRLBundleID] = provider.CRLData
2040			}
2041			providers = append(providers, provider)
2042		}
2043	
2044		if len(certBundles) == 0 {
2045			return providers, nil
2046		}
2047		return providers, certBundles
2048	}
2049	
2050	func telemetryEnabled(gw *graph.Gateway) bool {
2051		if gw == nil {
2052			return false
2053		}
2054	
2055		if gw.EffectiveNginxProxy == nil || gw.EffectiveNginxProxy.Telemetry == nil {
2056			return false
2057		}
2058	
2059		tel := gw.EffectiveNginxProxy.Telemetry
2060	
2061		if slices.Contains(tel.DisabledFeatures, ngfAPIv1alpha2.DisableTracing) {
2062			return false
2063		}
2064	
2065		if tel.Exporter == nil || tel.Exporter.Endpoint == nil {
2066			return false
2067		}
2068	
2069		return true
2070	}
2071	
2072	// buildTelemetry generates the Otel configuration.
2073	func buildTelemetry(g *graph.Graph, gateway *graph.Gateway) Telemetry {
2074		if !telemetryEnabled(gateway) {
2075			return Telemetry{}
2076		}
2077	
2078		serviceName := fmt.Sprintf("ngf:%s:%s", gateway.Source.Namespace, gateway.Source.Name)
2079		telemetry := gateway.EffectiveNginxProxy.Telemetry
2080		if telemetry.ServiceName != nil {
2081			serviceName = serviceName + ":" + *telemetry.ServiceName
2082		}
2083	
2084		tel := Telemetry{
2085			Endpoint:    *telemetry.Exporter.Endpoint, // safe to deref here since we verified that telemetry is enabled
2086			ServiceName: serviceName,
2087		}
2088	
2089		if telemetry.Exporter.BatchCount != nil {
2090			tel.BatchCount = *telemetry.Exporter.BatchCount
2091		}
2092		if telemetry.Exporter.BatchSize != nil {
2093			tel.BatchSize = *telemetry.Exporter.BatchSize
2094		}
2095		if telemetry.Exporter.Interval != nil {
2096			tel.Interval = string(*telemetry.Exporter.Interval)
2097		}
2098	
2099		tel.SpanAttributes = setSpanAttributes(telemetry.SpanAttributes)
2100	
2101		// FIXME(sberman): https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/issues/2038
2102		// Find a generic way to include relevant policy info at the http context so we don't need policy-specific
2103		// logic in this function
2104		ratioMap := make(map[string]int32)
2105		for _, pol := range g.NGFPolicies {
2106			if obsPol, ok := pol.Source.(*ngfAPIv1alpha2.ObservabilityPolicy); ok {
2107				if obsPol.Spec.Tracing != nil && obsPol.Spec.Tracing.Ratio != nil && *obsPol.Spec.Tracing.Ratio > 0 {
2108					ratioName := CreateRatioVarName(*obsPol.Spec.Tracing.Ratio)
2109					ratioMap[ratioName] = *obsPol.Spec.Tracing.Ratio
2110				}
2111			}
2112		}
2113	
2114		tel.Ratios = make([]Ratio, 0, len(ratioMap))
2115		for name, ratio := range ratioMap {
2116			tel.Ratios = append(tel.Ratios, Ratio{Name: name, Value: ratio})
2117		}
2118	
2119		return tel
2120	}
2121	
2122	func setSpanAttributes(spanAttributes []ngfAPIv1alpha1.SpanAttribute) []SpanAttribute {

2141	// buildBaseHTTPConfig generates the base http context config that should be applied to all servers.
2142	func buildBaseHTTPConfig(
2143		gateway *graph.Gateway,
2144		gatewaySnippetsFilters map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.SnippetsFilter,
2145		gatewayRateLimitPolicies map[graph.PolicyKey]*graph.Policy,
2146	) BaseHTTPConfig {
2147		baseConfig := BaseHTTPConfig{
2148			// HTTP2 should be enabled by default
2149			HTTP2:    true,
2150			IPFamily: Dual,
2151			Policies: buildPolicies(gateway, gateway.Policies),
2152			Snippets: buildSnippetsForContext(gatewaySnippetsFilters, ngfAPIv1alpha1.NginxContextHTTP),
2153		}
2154	
2155		// Create HTTP context policies for route-targeting RateLimitPolicies
2156		// For RateLimitPolicies that target routes that aren't attached to the Gateway,
2157		// these policies need to create the limit_req_zone directive at the http context.
2158		// To achieve this, we create a modified copy of the RateLimitPolicy with an annotation
2159		// indicating it's for HTTP context use only and attach it to the base HTTP config.
2160		httpContextRateLimitPolicies := buildHTTPContextRateLimitPolicies(gatewayRateLimitPolicies)
2161		baseConfig.Policies = append(baseConfig.Policies, httpContextRateLimitPolicies...)
2162	
2163		if gateway.Valid && gateway.SecretRef != nil {
2164			baseConfig.GatewaySecretID = generateSSLKeyPairID(*gateway.SecretRef)
2165		}
2166	
2167		// safe to access EffectiveNginxProxy since we only call this function when the Gateway is not nil.
2168		np := gateway.EffectiveNginxProxy
2169	
2170		// These helpers handle np == nil internally, so call them before the nil check.
2171		baseConfig.NginxReadinessProbePort = GetNginxReadinessProbePort(np)
2172		baseConfig.NginxReadinessProbePath = GetNginxReadinessProbePath(np)
2173	
2174		if np == nil {
2175			return baseConfig
2176		}
2177	
2178		if np.DisableHTTP2 != nil && *np.DisableHTTP2 {
2179			baseConfig.HTTP2 = false
2180		}
2181	
2182		if np.DisableSNIHostValidation != nil && *np.DisableSNIHostValidation {
2183			baseConfig.DisableSNIHostValidation = true
2184		}
2185	
2186		if np.IPFamily != nil {
2187			switch *np.IPFamily {
2188			case ngfAPIv1alpha2.IPv4:
2189				baseConfig.IPFamily = IPv4
2190			case ngfAPIv1alpha2.IPv6:
2191				baseConfig.IPFamily = IPv6
2192			}
2193		}
2194	
2195		baseConfig.RewriteClientIPSettings = buildRewriteClientIPConfig(np.RewriteClientIP)
2196	
2197		baseConfig.DNSResolver = buildDNSResolverConfig(np.DNSResolver)
2198	
2199		baseConfig.ServerTokens = buildServerTokens(gateway)
2200		baseConfig.DisableBaseProxySetHeaders = buildDisableBaseProxySetHeaders(np)
2201	
2202		baseConfig.Compression = buildCompressionConfig(np.Compression)
2203	
2204		return baseConfig
2205	}

2220	// buildHTTPContextRateLimitPolicies creates HTTP context versions of RateLimitPolicies that target routes.
2221	// These policies are modified copies with an annotation to indicate they're for HTTP context use only.
2222	func buildHTTPContextRateLimitPolicies(gatewayRateLimitPolicies map[graph.PolicyKey]*graph.Policy) []policies.Policy {
2223		if len(gatewayRateLimitPolicies) == 0 {
2224			return nil
2225		}
2226	
2227		httpContextRateLimitPolicies := make([]policies.Policy, 0, len(gatewayRateLimitPolicies))
2228	
2229		for _, graphPolicy := range gatewayRateLimitPolicies {
2230			if graphPolicy == nil || !graphPolicy.Valid {
2231				continue
2232			}
2233	
2234			// Extract the actual RateLimitPolicy from the graph.Policy wrapper
2235			rateLimitPolicy, ok := graphPolicy.Source.(*ngfAPIv1alpha1.RateLimitPolicy)
2236			if !ok {
2237				continue
2238			}
2239	
2240			// Create a deep copy of the RateLimitPolicy
2241			httpContextPolicy := rateLimitPolicy.DeepCopy()
2242	
2243			// Add a marker annotation to identify this as a fake HTTP context policy
2244			if httpContextPolicy.Annotations == nil {
2245				httpContextPolicy.Annotations = make(map[string]string)
2246			}
2247			httpContextPolicy.Annotations[InternalRLPAnnotationKey] = InternalRLPAnnotationValue
2248	
2249			// Convert to policies.Policy interface and add to the list
2250			httpContextRateLimitPolicies = append(httpContextRateLimitPolicies, httpContextPolicy)
2251		}
2252	
2253		// Sort for deterministic ordering
2254		sort.Slice(httpContextRateLimitPolicies, func(i, j int) bool {
2255			policyI, okI := httpContextRateLimitPolicies[i].(*ngfAPIv1alpha1.RateLimitPolicy)
2256			policyJ, okJ := httpContextRateLimitPolicies[j].(*ngfAPIv1alpha1.RateLimitPolicy)
2257	
2258			if !okI || !okJ {
2259				// If type cast fails, fall back to comparing by string representation
2260				return fmt.Sprintf("%v", httpContextRateLimitPolicies[i]) < fmt.Sprintf("%v", httpContextRateLimitPolicies[j])
2261			}
2262	
2263			if policyI.Namespace != policyJ.Namespace {
2264				return policyI.Namespace < policyJ.Namespace
2265			}
2266			return policyI.Name < policyJ.Name
2267		})
2268	
2269		return httpContextRateLimitPolicies
2270	}

2570	// resolveUpstreamEndpoints handles service resolution for both regular and ExternalName services.
2571	func resolveUpstreamEndpoints(
2572		ctx context.Context,
2573		logger logr.Logger,
2574		br graph.BackendRef,
2575		svcResolver resolver.ServiceResolver,
2576		referencedServices map[types.NamespacedName]*graph.ReferencedService,
2577	) ([]resolver.Endpoint, error) {
2578		// Check if this is an ExternalName service
2579		if externalName := getExternalHostname(br.SvcNsName, referencedServices); externalName != "" {
2580			// For ExternalName services, create an endpoint directly with the external name
2581			endpoint := resolver.Endpoint{
2582				Address: externalName,
2583				Port:    br.ServicePort.Port,
2584				IPv6:    false, // DNS names are neither IPv4 nor IPv6
2585				Resolve: true,  // ExternalName services require DNS resolution
2586			}
2587	
2588			logger.V(1).Info("resolved ExternalName service",
2589				"service", br.SvcNsName,
2590				"externalName", externalName,
2591				"port", br.ServicePort.Port)
2592	
2593			return []resolver.Endpoint{endpoint}, nil
2594		}
2595	
2596		// Resolve endpoints for both IPv4 and IPv6. NginxProxy ipFamily controls only the
2597		// NGINX listen directives, not which upstream endpoints are selected.
2598		return svcResolver.Resolve(
2599			ctx,
2600			logger,
2601			br.SvcNsName,
2602			br.ServicePort,
2603			[]discoveryV1.AddressType{discoveryV1.AddressTypeIPv4, discoveryV1.AddressTypeIPv6},
2604		)
2605	}

2668	func buildWAF(gateway *graph.Gateway) WAFConfig {

2670		wb := convertWAFBundles(gatewayBundles)

2673		if gateway.Source != nil && !graph.WAFCookieSeedDisabledForNginxProxy(gateway.EffectiveNginxProxy) {
2674			cookieSeed = string(gateway.Source.UID)
2675		}
2676	
2677		wc := WAFConfig{
2678			Enabled:    graph.WAFEnabledForNginxProxy(gateway.EffectiveNginxProxy),

2685	// collectGatewayWAFBundles collects WAF bundles from all WAFPolicies that target
2686	// this gateway directly or target routes attached to this gateway.
2687	func collectGatewayWAFBundles(gateway *graph.Gateway) map[graph.WAFBundleKey]*graph.WAFBundleData {
2688		bundles := make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]*graph.WAFBundleData)
2689	
2690		// Collect bundles from policies targeting the gateway directly.
2691		for _, policy := range gateway.Policies {
2692			if policy.WAFState == nil {
2693				continue
2694			}
2695	
2696			maps.Copy(bundles, policy.WAFState.Bundles)
2697		}
2698	
2699		// Collect bundles from policies targeting routes attached to this gateway.
2700		for _, listener := range gateway.Listeners {
2701			for _, route := range listener.Routes {
2702				for _, policy := range route.Policies {
2703					if policy.WAFState == nil {
2704						continue
2705					}
2706	
2707					maps.Copy(bundles, policy.WAFState.Bundles)
2708				}
2709			}
2710		}
2711	
2712		return bundles
2713	}
